name: CRUD/resource-type-case-sensitivity
description: Conformance tests ported from Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E Rest/ResourceTypeCaseSensitivityTests.cs: FHIR resource type names in the URL path are case-sensitive per the R4 specification, so a mis-cased type segment (e.g. 'patient' or 'PATIENT' instead of 'Patient') must be rejected with 404 Not Found across every HTTP method (read, type-level search, update, delete, create, patch), while the correctly-cased type routes successfully. No fixtures, setup, or teardown are required since no resources are created.

test.name: mis-cased resource type segments are rejected with 404 across all HTTP methods
test.description: GET/PUT/DELETE/POST/PATCH requests using a lowercase ('patient') or uppercase ('PATIENT') resource type segment instead of the correctly-cased 'Patient' must all return 404 Not Found, since FHIR resource type names are case-sensitive.

test.name: correctly-cased resource type routes successfully
test.description: GET /Patient (correctly-cased type-level search) must route successfully and return HTTP 200 with a Bundle, proving the case-sensitivity rejections above are specific to the mis-cased segment rather than a general routing failure.
